summaries.167798: The issue is reproducible on iOS device (not simulator) on both stable 3.29.3 and master channel. A commenter identified the root cause: `bottomBound` in `RenderEditable._calculateAdjustedCursorOffset` becomes negative when the TextField is given a constrained height via `contentPadding`, causing the `clampDouble` assertion to fire. The proposed fix is to always use `_textPainter.height` when computing `bottomBound`. Assignee was removed after no progress; a later check found it still reproduces on Flutter 3.40.0-1.0.pre.344.
summaries.51257: The root cause is that the TextField's `InputDecorator` claims more vertical padding than the given height allows, causing `RenderEditable` to draw outside its bounds silently until text overflows and clipping kicks in. Workarounds confirmed: `isDense: true`, reducing `contentPadding` (including `EdgeInsets.zero` or `isCollapsed: true`). An initial overflow-warning fix landed via #55408 but did not fully resolve the issue; PR #63639 implementing a debug overflow indicator was subsequently reverted as #64573 due to a blocking issue (#48679). A later comment notes the same clipping also occurs with `OutlineInputBorder` without constraining height. Currently unassigned and waiting for a contributor to submit a fix.
summaries.96660: Confirmed reproducible on stable 2.8.1, master, and beta 2.10 on iOS simulator and on physical iPad/iPhone devices with an external keyboard. A commenter adds that holding Delete key and Arrow keys on a real iPad also does not repeat. The issue is iOS-specific and appears to affect holding any hardware keyboard key, not just Backspace. No fix or PR referenced; status is open and stalled.
summaries.123065: Comments confirm the issue also affects Japanese text identically. Platform breakdown: Flutter web already handles CJK word breaking correctly (uses the engine's `word_breaker.dart` which implements full Unicode TR29 rules); macOS/Windows/Linux move only one character at a time. Commenters discuss using the engine's web `WordBreaker` as a shared implementation, and CLDR-based script-property rules (e.g., `ja.xml` Hiragana/Ideographic rules) as a dictionary-free approximation for CJK. A commenter notes that newer ICU data tables adopted for desktop may make proper handling more feasible, and that the same problem affects word selection by double-tap/long-press on mobile. No PR; status is open and stalled.
summaries.180435: Comments identify two distinct bugs. First, `DoNothingAction` cannot override `Action.overridable` because `Actions._castAction` does an `is Action<T>` check that fails for `DoNothingAction` (Dart's reified generics are invariant). Workaround: create a typed `MyDoNothingAction<T extends Intent>`. Second, `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentLineIntent` and `ExtendSelectionVerticallyToAdjacentPageIntent` cannot be individually overridden because the underlying action in `editable_text.dart` is typed to `DirectionalCaretMovementIntent`; PR #180883 may have addressed this. A commenter proposes fixing `_castAction` to allow `Action<Intent>` to pass the type check. Status: has partial fix in #180883; root `DoNothingAction` issue open.
summaries.136419: Deprecations were committed (#136677), starting the one-year countdown. Comments document migration pain points: `RawKeyEventDataLinux.scanCode` has no equivalent in the new API (a user worked around it with a manual USB HID→scan-code mapping); modifier-mask-based keybinding logic must be rewritten using `HardwareKeyboard.isMetaPressed` etc. A web-specific issue was raised where `KeyboardListener` does not fire events (filed separately). A game-dev team reported a blocking issue (#162305) on web and requested the removal be delayed; a macOS regression was also reported (#148936). The `simulateKeyDownEvent`/`simulateKeyUpEvent` and `KeyEventSimulator` items remain unchecked. Status: deprecations landed; removal timeline slipped past Q4 2024 due to blocking issues.
summaries.80108: Comments confirm the mismatch is reproducible on stable 2.0.4 and master 2.1.0 on Windows. The logs show that a barcode scanner sends shift+digit key sequences, which Flutter maps to shifted logical keys (e.g., `!` for digit 1) on the first event, then emits a second event with an unknown Windows key code (e.g., `Unknown Windows key code 49`) on the same physical key. No fix or PR referenced; status is stalled, triaged to team-windows.
summaries.110788: Comments confirm the issue also affects `SelectionArea` on both beta and master channels on iOS and Android. When "Select All" is used inside a `ListView`, only the currently visible text is selected. A simplified repro confirms the selection resets when the list scrolls. No fix or PR referenced; status is open and stalled.
summaries.119050: Triage confirms the white-space flicker after keyboard dismissal reproduces on master (3.7.0-26.0.pre.15) on both iOS and Android (HTML and CanvasKit renderers); the lag is described as taking a couple of seconds for the UI to update. The issue was filed January 2023 and commenters note it is still unresolved as of early 2024, citing lack of web team attention. No fix or PR referenced; status is stalled waiting on team-web.
summaries.130559: The issue is specific to typing Korean past the `maxLength` limit and then pressing Backspace (not Delete, not Space, not Backspace after moving the cursor). Confirmed reproducible on Windows stable 3.10.6 and master 3.13 by a second tester after initial failure to reproduce. Not reproducible on macOS. Web has a related but distinct problem where Korean can exceed `maxLength` at all. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.136745: Comments confirm reproduction on physical Android devices with an external keyboard (Realme 6, Pixel Tablet) and also on emulator. Root cause analysis from a commenter: `View.onFocusChanged()` in Android calls `notifyFocusChangeToImeFocusController(true)` on any focusable view gaining focus; if the window has IME focus, `ViewRootImpl` eventually shows the keyboard. This is deep in the Android view system and cannot be intercepted from the SDK. Android team marked it P2 and deprioritized for Q4. Still reproducible on Flutter 3.29.3. No fix or PR; status is stalled.
summaries.70841: Comments confirm the double-caret issue is specific to old Safari (iOS 9.x) and does not reproduce on Safari 14 / iPad 8. The root cause is that Flutter renders its own cursor while the browser also shows a native cursor, and they get out of sync. No workaround or fix was offered; a question about whether hiding the browser cursor via `index.html` would help was raised but not answered. Status: open, old OS-version-specific, no PR.
summaries.107350: Comments clarify that `AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction` on a `Form` validates all fields on any interaction with any field, which is the original design (confirmed by the PR #59766 test). A commenter proposes a new mode `AutovalidateMode.singleOnUserInteractionForm`, but ultimately the decision was to change the behavior of the existing mode to only validate the interacted field. A partial patch exists in PR #120830; a follow-up fix was planned pending PR #180822. Status: has partial patch, waiting for follow-up PR.
summaries.148151: Comments confirm reproduction on stable 3.22.0 and master with a physical iPhone. A second user reports the prompt always appears when the keyboard is dismissed (not when the button is clicked), and a third user notes the same with multiple password fields on a signup page. No root cause, fix, or PR identified; status is stalled with no team response.
summaries.106923: Discussion confirms Flutter has no built-in mechanism for shared/retained focus across overlay widgets whose focus nodes cannot be placed as descendants of the editor's focus node in the widget tree. @LongCatIsLooong explains `hasFocus` is true as long as a descendant has focus, but overlays break that hierarchy. @gspencergoog confirms there is no focus-stack concept and manual `FocusNode` parenting outside `Focus` widgets is fragile. PR #113655 added optional `parentNode` to `Focus`/`FocusScope`, but subsequent comments show `DropdownButton` still steals focus because it forces its own route. Status: partial fix landed in #113655; `DropdownButton` in overlays still broken; stalled.
summaries.114254: Comments identify the root cause: during tab focus traversal, `ReadingOrderTraversalPolicy.sortDescendants` collects focus nodes from all `Navigator` pages including inactive ones; since inactive pages are not laid out, their `RenderBox.size` assertion fires. The fix is to exclude inactive Navigator pages from focus traversal. A commenter confirms the original reporter may have worked around it differently and the issue may still be open. Status: root cause identified, no fix merged.
summaries.143047: Comments confirm the crash reproduces on Android (stable 3.16 and master). The crash occurs in two stages: first, `NaN` is serialized when `setEditableSizeAndTransform` is called on a TextField not yet in the viewport (because the preceding sliver has infinite height); second, the viewport layout asserts `correctedOffset.isFinite`. A commenter argues this is an invalid widget tree (a sliver of infinite height followed by another sliver is unreachable by scrolling) and suggests an assert should be added via PR #141671. A later comment reports inability to reproduce on stable 3.27.2 on Android. Status: disputed validity; no fix landed.
